qa_pairs.question: What is the policy number for this term with riders policy?
qa_pairs.evidence: Policy Number: PP-TL-2024-6729R

qa_pairs.question: What is the face amount of the base policy?
qa_pairs.evidence: Face Amount: $750,000

qa_pairs.question: What is the total annual premium including all riders?
qa_pairs.evidence: Total Annual Premium (including riders): $1,089.00

qa_pairs.question: What is the base annual premium excluding riders?
qa_pairs.evidence: Base Annual Premium: $612.00

qa_pairs.question: What is the policy term?
qa_pairs.evidence: Policy Period: 30 years (expires July 12, 2054)

qa_pairs.question: What is the insured's underwriting class?
qa_pairs.evidence: Underwriting Class: Preferred Non-Smoker

qa_pairs.question: What is the annual premium for the Waiver of Premium rider?
qa_pairs.evidence: RIDER 1: WAIVER OF PREMIUM DUE TO DISABILITY / Premium: $102/year

qa_pairs.question: What is the annual premium for the Children's Term Insurance Rider?
qa_pairs.evidence: RIDER 4: CHILDREN'S TERM INSURANCE RIDER / Premium: $156/year

qa_pairs.question: What coverage amount does the Children's Term Insurance Rider provide per child?
qa_pairs.evidence: Provides $15,000 coverage on each eligible child

qa_pairs.question: What is the additional accidental death benefit amount?
qa_pairs.evidence: RIDER 5: ACCIDENTAL DEATH BENEFIT / Pays an additional $750,000 if death results from accidental injury

qa_pairs.question: What is the annual premium for the Accidental Death Benefit rider?
qa_pairs.evidence: RIDER 5: ACCIDENTAL DEATH BENEFIT / Premium: $94/year

qa_pairs.question: What is the grace period for premium payments?
qa_pairs.evidence: 3.2 GRACE PERIOD: 31 days from due date. Coverage continues during grace period.

qa_pairs.question: What is the maximum additional coverage purchasable per option date under the Guaranteed Insurability Option rider?
qa_pairs.evidence: May purchase up to $100,000 additional coverage per option date

qa_pairs.question: At what age does the Accidental Death Benefit rider terminate?
qa_pairs.evidence: Terminates at insured's age 70

qa_pairs.question: What is the annual premium for the Guaranteed Insurability Option rider?
qa_pairs.evidence: RIDER 6: GUARANTEED INSURABILITY OPTION / Premium: $89/year

qa_pairs.question: What is the policy date?
qa_pairs.evidence: Policy Date: July 12, 2024

qa_pairs.question: Who is the insured?
qa_pairs.evidence: Name: Christopher David Bennett

qa_pairs.question: Who is the primary beneficiary and what are their respective shares?
qa_pairs.answer: Rachel Marie Bennett (Wife) at 60%, Christopher Bennett Jr. (Son) at 20%, and Olivia Grace Bennett (Daughter) at 20%
qa_pairs.evidence: Primary: Rachel Marie Bennett (Wife) - 60% / Christopher Bennett Jr. (Son) - 20% / Olivia Grace Bennett (Daughter) - 20%

qa_pairs.question: Who is the contingent beneficiary?
qa_pairs.evidence: Contingent: Bennett Family Irrevocable Trust - 100%

qa_pairs.question: What children are covered under the Children's Term Insurance Rider?
qa_pairs.answer: Christopher Bennett Jr. (Age 8) and Olivia Grace Bennett (Age 5)
qa_pairs.evidence: Covered Children: Christopher Bennett Jr. (Age 8) / Olivia Grace Bennett (Age 5)

qa_pairs.question: What company issued this policy?
qa_pairs.evidence: ProtectPlus Family Insurance Co.

qa_pairs.question: What are the Guaranteed Insurability Option exercise dates?
qa_pairs.answer: At ages 40, 43, 46, 49, and at the birth or adoption of a child
qa_pairs.evidence: Option dates: Ages 40, 43, 46, 49 and at birth/adoption of child

qa_pairs.question: If the insured becomes totally disabled before age 60 and remains disabled for 6 months, what happens to premiums?
qa_pairs.answer: All future premiums are waived and coverage continues in full force.
qa_pairs.evidence: If you become totally disabled before age 60 and remain disabled for 6 consecutive months: All future premiums are waived / Coverage continues in full force

qa_pairs.question: What is paid if the insured dies by suicide within 2 years of the issue date?
qa_pairs.answer: Only the premiums paid are returned, without interest.
qa_pairs.evidence: 1.3 EXCLUSION - SUICIDE: If death occurs by suicide within 2 years of the issue date, we will pay only the premiums paid, without interest.

qa_pairs.question: If an unpaid premium is due when the insured dies during the grace period, is it deducted from the death benefit?
qa_pairs.answer: Yes. If death occurs during the grace period, the unpaid premium will be deducted from the death benefit.
qa_pairs.evidence: 3.2 GRACE PERIOD: 31 days from due date. Coverage continues during grace period. If death occurs during grace period, unpaid premium will be deducted from death benefit.

qa_pairs.question: Under what condition is aviation death covered under the base policy for this insured?
qa_pairs.answer: Aviation death is covered because the standard aviation exclusion has been waived based on the insured's declared private pilot activities, with an additional premium of $175/year included. The only remaining exception is military combat operations.
qa_pairs.evidence: SECTION 6: AVIATION EXCLUSION WAIVER: The standard aviation exclusion has been waived for this policy based on the insured's declared private pilot activities. Additional premium of $175/year is included in the base premium. Coverage includes death while: Operating or riding as a passenger in any aircraft / Exception: military combat operations

qa_pairs.question: Under what conditions does the Accidental Death Benefit not pay?
qa_pairs.answer: The rider excludes: suicide, self-inflicted injury, war, aviation (as pilot/crew), and death if the insured was under the influence of drugs or alcohol.
qa_pairs.evidence: Excludes: suicide, self-inflicted injury, war, aviation (as pilot/crew) / No payment if insured was under influence of drugs/alcohol

qa_pairs.question: When do all Guaranteed Insurability Option rights terminate?
qa_pairs.evidence: All options terminate at age 50

qa_pairs.question: Until what age does the Children's Term Insurance Rider cover each child?
qa_pairs.evidence: Coverage from birth (or adoption) to age 25

qa_pairs.question: When may this policy be converted to permanent insurance without evidence of insurability?
qa_pairs.answer: Before the policy expiration date or before the insured's 70th birthday, whichever is earlier, with a minimum conversion amount of $50,000.
qa_pairs.evidence: 4.2 You may convert all or part of this policy to permanent life insurance without evidence of insurability: Before the policy expiration date, or / Before your 70th birthday, whichever is earlier / Minimum conversion amount: $50,000

qa_pairs.question: If the insured dies in an aircraft accident as a private pilot, do both the base policy death benefit and the Accidental Death Benefit rider pay?
qa_pairs.answer: The base policy pays the $750,000 death benefit because the aviation exclusion was waived. However, the Accidental Death Benefit rider excludes aviation deaths as pilot or crew, so the additional $750,000 rider benefit would not be paid.
qa_pairs.evidence: SECTION 6: AVIATION EXCLUSION WAIVER: The standard aviation exclusion has been waived for this policy... Coverage includes death while: Operating or riding as a passenger in any aircraft / RIDER 5: Excludes: suicide, self-inflicted injury, war, aviation (as pilot/crew)

qa_pairs.question: If the insured is diagnosed with a terminal illness and accesses the Terminal Illness Accelerated Death Benefit, how much can be advanced and what is left for beneficiaries?
qa_pairs.answer: Up to 95% of the death benefit ($712,500) can be advanced (minimum $10,000, maximum $500,000), with the advance reduced by an actuarial discount. The remaining death benefit is paid to beneficiaries upon death.
qa_pairs.evidence: RIDER 2: ACCELERATED DEATH BENEFIT - TERMINAL ILLNESS / May access up to 95% of death benefit while living / Minimum advance: $10,000 / Maximum advance: $500,000 / Benefit reduced by actuarial discount (approximately 8% per year) / Remaining death benefit paid to beneficiaries upon death

qa_pairs.question: A child covered under the Children's Term Insurance Rider turns 25. Can they obtain individual life insurance coverage without a medical exam?
qa_pairs.answer: Yes. Children may convert to an individual policy at age 25 without evidence of insurability, for up to 5 times the rider amount ($75,000).
qa_pairs.evidence: Children may convert to individual policy at age 25 without evidence of insurability, up to 5 times the rider amount

qa_pairs.question: What is the Chronic Illness rider benefit amount per month as a percentage and what is the maximum total advance?
qa_pairs.answer: The rider allows access to up to 2% of the face amount per month, with a maximum total advance of 50% of the face amount.
qa_pairs.evidence: RIDER 3: May access up to 2% of face amount per month / Maximum total advance: 50% of face amount
